summary.intent: Fix a bug in the moltis project where custom OpenAI-compatible providers with explicit models configured still fire unnecessary /v1/models HTTP discovery requests (GitHub issue #504).
summary.outcome: Fixed the bug by adding an explicit model check before firing discovery in `fire_discoveries()`, added 3 regression tests, all 239 tests pass, code formatted and linted clean, committed and pushed with a PR created.

repo: Custom providers are identified by the 'custom-' name prefix in the providers config map.
Built-in OpenAI-compat providers have a `skip_discovery` guard that custom providers lacked — this was the root asymmetry causing the bug.
`configured_models_for_provider(config, name)` is the canonical way to check if a provider has explicit models set.
`merge_preferred_and_discovered_models` already handles the case of empty discovery results correctly — the fix only needed to touch the discovery-firing logic.
Model IDs include the provider prefix in the format `custom-<name>::<model-id>` when registered in the registry.
The crate has 238 pre-existing tests; the worktree path is `/Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/broken-daphne/`.

code.finding: The custom providers loop in `fire_discoveries()` lacked a guard for explicit models; fix adds `!configured_models_for_provider(config, name).is_empty()` check before calling `should_fetch_models`.
Built-in OpenAI-compat providers already have a `skip_discovery` guard — the pattern to replicate for custom providers.

workflow: When adding imports inside a test module that uses `use super::*`, prefer using full paths to avoid `unused qualification` lint errors on pre-existing code.
Editing around existing `#[test]` attributes requires care — it's easy to accidentally remove an attribute from the function immediately following the insertion point.
Clippy failures from CUDA/platform-specific features (not available on macOS) can be safely isolated by running clippy scoped to the changed crate only.

friction: Accidentally introduced a duplicate `#[test]` attribute when inserting the new test adjacent to an existing test, requiring multiple edits to fix.

Adding a `use` import for `ProviderEntry` inside the test module triggered `unused qualification` lint errors on pre-existing code that already used full paths — had to remove the import and switch to full paths in test code.
During duplicate `#[test]` cleanup, accidentally removed the `#[test]` attribute from the pre-existing `supports_reasoning_for_known_models` test, caught only via clippy.
Model ID assertion in the test initially failed because the registered ID includes the provider prefix (`custom-mylocal::my-model`) which wasn't obvious upfront.
open_items: Clippy reports a pre-existing CUDA build failure unrelated to this change — that underlying issue is unresolved.
The fix only addresses the discovery-firing path; no audit was done of whether other built-in provider guards (beyond `skip_discovery`) are also missing from the custom provider code path.
